title: AttentionDecisionV2
description: I-010B AttentionDecisionV2@2 — the tagged host-facing decision union, encoding the design's AttentionResponse union verbatim (FR-014, c834e8c). status 'ok' carries one of exactly four permitted classifier/effective pairs (SUPPRESS->SUPPRESS, SUPPRESS->DEFER, WAKE->WAKE, DEFER->DEFER) with the closed `routing_audit` (applied valve, override cause, margin status, effective margin exactly when the margin applied, trusted margin source only on a margin-applied decision), the sibling ok-branch 'reasons' audit field, evidence IDs, `classifier` audit naming the classifier with optional provider and model, the optional `legacy_verdict_confidences` vector — required exactly when the classifier disposition is SUPPRESS while the routing audit reports the margin active (FR-007) — and WAKE-only grounded `attention_advice` array (FR-013); status 'bypass' carries exactly cause 'preattention-disabled' and excludes the full FR-005 set (classifier/effective disposition, classifier audit, reasons, evidence, legacy confidence vector, routing audit, advice); status 'error' remains operational and separate, with the request ID optional on both the pre-validation and post-validation branches and an optional classifier audit present only when the error occurred after classifier invocation (FR-005, FR-014). Advice citations against the request's event IDs are a runtime-adapter-only rule (FR-013). @2 (post-acceptance amendment A2, evidence/v2/attention/dependency-010-amendment-A1-post-acceptance-zero-margin-blocker.md) widens routing_audit.effective_margin's domain from the accepted @1's wrongly exclusive (0,1] to the authority's inclusive [0,1]: a margin-defer decision with effective_margin exactly 0 is valid.

confidence.$comment: Finite [0,1]. The contradictory-bounds not-clause rejects NaN, which vacuously satisfies every numeric comparison keyword; strict JSON cannot carry non-finite literals, but sentinel-decoded corpus instances can.

attentionAdvice.$comment: Classifier-authored, non-authoritative annotation grounded in named evidence events; never a system instruction.

evidence_event_ids.$comment: Every citation must reference an event ID supplied in the request; that cross-document rule is runtime-adapter-only (FR-012/FR-013).

routingAudit.$comment: The closed FR-005 routing audit with its per-combination rules: a margin counts as applied exactly on valve margin-defer — the effective margin is then required and is forbidden on every other valve, override cause must be margin, and the margin status must be active (a retired margin cannot apply); the trusted margin source may appear only on that margin-applied decision (optional there); valves none and classifier-defer pair with override cause none; valve policy-defer pairs with override cause suppression-disabled or recoverability-unproven.

effective_margin.$comment: Finite [0, 1] (@2 amendment A2, c834e8c 'a transition margin, when active, is a finite number within [0,1]'; the accepted @1 shape wrongly used exclusiveMinimum, excluding the exact boundary the retained inclusive <= comparison relies on). The contradictory-bounds not-clause rejects NaN exactly as $defs/confidence does.

reasons.$comment: A required sibling ok-branch audit field — never a member of the routing-audit object — retained as audit material that never enters the participant turn.

legacy_verdict_confidences.$comment: FR-007: optional on status ok and required exactly when the classifier disposition is SUPPRESS while the routing audit reports the margin active; a well-formed vector may accompany WAKE, DEFER, or a margin-retired SUPPRESS without invalidating them. When present: exactly the four legacy verdict keys with finite values in [0,1]. The optional field, its exact shape, and the conditional requirement were fixed as of @1 and remain unchanged through @2 (amendment A2 touches only routing_audit.effective_margin); reshaping this field's own shape would itself be a further breaking edit.

allOf.$comment: FR-007 conditional requirement: a margin-active candidate SUPPRESS must carry the legacy vector.

oneOf.$comment: WAKE->WAKE: valve none (no widening valve applied); the only pair that may carry advice (FR-013).
DEFER->DEFER: the classifier-defer valve, distinct from margin-defer (S08).
SUPPRESS->DEFER: widened by the margin or policy valve; the exact valve, override cause, and margin facts follow the routing cross-field rules.
SUPPRESS->SUPPRESS: governed suppression with no widening valve applied (S05).

bypass.$comment: A non-social no-classifier outcome excluding the full FR-005 set: no classifier/effective disposition, classifier audit, reasons, evidence, legacy confidence vector, routing audit, or advice. Not a successful disposition pairing; it must not fabricate a model judgment.

error.$comment: The tagged operational branch: malformed output, illegal pairings, and invalid legacy confidence land here and cannot fabricate a social suppression. request_id is optional on both the pre-validation and post-validation branches (a pre-validation error may occur before a request ID is assignable); classifier is optional and present only when the error occurred after classifier invocation. code is the authority's open string (FR-014, rejection R7) — not a locally narrowed enum; detail is required (the authority's complete error object is {code, detail}, both required).
